title: Add semantic project validation and deterministic sync
description: Extend health checks beyond record shape and make routine regeneration reliable. Default validation should report readiness inconsistencies. Project validation should check safe literal path hints and generated artifact freshness using the caller project root. A sync command should validate canonical records, rebuild the index, regenerate selected reports/views, and verify project health in a deterministic order.
acceptance: Default validation warns when a declared-ready task has unmet dependencies and does not warn for done/wont_do dependencies.
validate --project warns for literal relative path hints that escape or do not exist under the caller project root; planned missing paths remain warnings, not errors.
validate --project detects stale STATUS, active-work, blocked, and requested task-view output without mutating files.
Every generated Markdown artifact ends with exactly one LF and passes byte-level regression tests plus git diff --check.
Reindex JSON and human output report claims_total and claims_active separately.
waystation sync [--views] stops on canonical errors, then reindexes, regenerates, and runs project validation in order.
Sync uses a consistent canonical snapshot or detects concurrent canonical changes and refuses to certify mixed derived output.
Two sync runs without canonical changes produce byte-identical derived output and no unexplained drift.
notes: The generated-artifact policy decision precedes this task and is an input to freshness and sync behavior. Keep validation read-only, generated artifacts derived and idempotent, and literal-path semantics explicit.
